content.recommended_action: Approve only a limited, conditional chase rather than the full 20,000 units. Bias any reorder toward the clearly constrained pink color and away from navy, and avoid overcommitting because demand is partially contaminated by abnormal returns and timing risk. Require size-curve correction for sizes 7/8 and 10/12 before placement, and treat any units landing after peak cold-weather week as higher markdown risk. If the vendor cannot support corrected sizing, color mix skew to pink, and a smaller or cancelable commitment, do not approve the chase.
evidence_trusted: Pink is 82 percent sold through, indicating likely genuine scarcity in at least one color.
Navy is only 39 percent sold through, showing demand is highly uneven by color and the style is not broadly clean.
Return rate is 18 percent versus 9 percent norm, with fit-too-small concentrated in sizes 7/8 and 10/12, indicating sizing issues are materially distorting apparent demand.
Lead time lands after peak cold-weather week, reducing the value of a large chase and increasing markdown exposure.
Vendor is requesting a 20,000 unit commitment by tomorrow, creating pressure for a fast decision despite meaningful uncertainty.
evidence_discounted: The 2.4M views signal attention but are not treated as clean conversion demand on their own.
High sell-through in pink is partially discounted because elevated returns suggest some sales may reverse or reflect poor fit rather than durable demand.
Aggregate style-level momentum is discounted because weak navy performance suggests the viral signal does not apply uniformly across variants.
Any implied urgency from social virality is discounted because late delivery after the key weather window limits commercial upside.
constraints: Cannot treat current demand as clean because returns are elevated and linked to fit problems.
Need to avoid optimizing only for viral views or pink sell-through while ignoring weak navy productivity and timing risk.
Lead time arriving after peak cold-weather week materially limits full-price sell-through potential.
Decision value is large and downside is high, so irreversible full-scale commitments are inappropriate without caveats.
Size issues in 7/8 and 10/12 must be addressed in the buy plan or fit spec before reorder.
uncertainty: Unknown how much of pink demand remains after adjusting for returns and fit-related dissatisfaction.
Unknown whether sizing can be corrected in time for the chase order.
Unknown whether the vendor can accept a reduced quantity, revised size curve, or flexible/cancelable terms.
Unknown remaining cold-weather demand after the peak week passes.
Unknown profitability impact from potential markdowns and reverse logistics if a large late order is placed.

content.decision: Do not commit to all three proposed new barrier serum SKUs at the proposed scale. Proceed only with a limited test architecture of up to two SKUs if a materially smaller, reversible buy is possible within OTB; otherwise defer launch until funding/facings are resolved.

recommended_action.sku_architecture: Maximum 2 SKUs, not 3, because only 4 facings are open and each SKU requires 2 facings.
recommended_action.scale: Small test only, sized to available OTB or contingent on reduced initial commitments/vendor flexibility; do not place the $52,800 buy against $11,800 OTB.
test_design: Select 2 differentiated SKUs only if they cover distinct use cases and avoid redundant shades/formats.
Use launch as a controlled read rather than a chainwide scale commitment.
Require reorder-based expansion only after observed sell-through, conversion after price reveal, and sustained demand post-competitor restock.
commercial_guardrails: Seek lower MOQ, split shipment, or consignment/dated cancellation terms before any commitment.
Preserve OTB for core replenishment and higher-confidence opportunities.
Do not add a third SKU unless additional facings open and demand is validated.

evidence_trusted: Physical space is a hard constraint: 4 facings open and each SKU needs 2, so only 2 SKUs fit.
Current OTB after core replenishment is $11,800, far below the proposed buy cost of $52,800.
Core replenishment already has priority claim on budget.
evidence_discounted: Prestige competitor sellout may reflect temporary stockout or channel-specific demand and should not be treated as clean transferable demand.
Waitlist of 1,900 is before price reveal, so conversion is uncertain.
Same-retailer adjacent serum comps are mixed, so extrapolation is unreliable.

2 facings required per SKU

Proposed buy cost: $52,800

Medium downside from error

uncertainty.financial_risk: High if full proposed buy is placed because commitment materially exceeds available OTB.

content.tradeoff_summary: If OTB is $2.1M, approving all three leaves only $50k headroom and effectively consumes the full balance, which is risky for remaining holiday needs. If OTB is $2.4M, all three fit with $350k headroom, but the source conflict must be resolved first. Outerwear appears most time-critical due to next-Friday deadline and stockout risk; Beauty has vendor support that may improve economics; Toys has demand upside tied to a viral trend but may carry higher trend risk.

content.margin_read: At regular price, gross margin dollars are about $26.49 per case ($39.99 - $13.50). Since prior 20% standalone promotion lifted units but reduced attachment quality, a full 20% discount risks paying away too much margin for lower-value or less incremental attachments. Tablet margin is unaffected, so decision should focus on whether attachment-rate gain justifies lower case margin; a smaller discount better protects margin.
content.demand_read: Current attachment rate is 22% versus 30% target, so there is room to stimulate attachment demand. With 7 weeks of inventory cover, there is no urgent need to clear stock aggressively.
risk_controls: Do not run the full 20% discount broadly without a test/control read on incremental attach improvement.
Use a shallower 10-15% attachment-only offer tied explicitly to tablet purchase.
Track incremental attachment rate, margin dollars per tablet sold, and promo redemption quality versus baseline.
Set a time-boxed test and stop if attachment gains do not offset margin dilution.
Avoid aggressive promotion beyond attachment context given only moderate inventory cover and no clearance need.
